mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.8 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 52 percent over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real personal savings rates indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both INDPRO and RSAFS decline concurrently, or the combined predictive power does not exceed the sum of individual series predictive power by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a month-over-month decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader credit tightening and consumer demand contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not produce statistically significant negative 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces corporate revenue expectations and reduces recession probabilities, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the sign of the forward return distribution flips relative to payroll growth direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 245.0, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL breaches 245.0, or the signal hit rate drops below 50 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real personal savings rates fall while unemployment remains stable indicates households are drawing down buffers to sustain consumption, which historically precedes a demand shock that negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and stable UNRATE does not predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating economic growth, a contraction in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks that cap corporate margin expansion, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent annualized
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high GDP growth periods do not produce negative 5-day forward returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
